sourceFile: 9. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B2 (50 Questions) R01.pdf
question: The Leap -1A cowling assembly consists of: (CAT A, B1, B2)

options.text: Air intake cowl, fan cowl, thrust reverser cowl, exhaust system.
Drain mast.
Engine build up.

sourceFile: 9. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B2 (50 Questions) R01.pdf
question: How to deactivate thrust reverser system for maintenance (Eng Leap 1-A): (CAT A, B1, B2)

options.text: Pull the lock handle at manual locking actuator.
With Lockout Pin with red warning flag
Install the quick-release pin to hold the inhibit lever in the OFF position.

sourceFile: 9. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B2 (50 Questions) R01.pdf
question: How to make sure Engine Leap 1-A Fan cowl door fully locked: (CAT A, B1, B2)

options.text: Make sure 03 latch handles are fully locked and flush with the fan cowl surface and the flag on the LH Fan cowl door is fully retracted.
Make sure that all the latch handles stay in the fan cowl slots and are aligned with the adjacent fan cowl surface. And the key removed after turnback to lock position.

Use a SCREWDRIVER to push the pin towards the door locator then make sure that all the latches stay in the fan cowl slots and are aligned with the bottom of the adjacent cowl surface and the key removed.

sourceFile: 9. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B2 (50 Questions) R01.pdf
question: If you see the red flag shown on the Eng Leap 1-A LH Fan cowl door, what does it mean: (CAT A, B1, B2)

options.text: Fan cowl latch keeper No.1 opened (AMM 71-13-00-00 CONF 03)
All Reverser latches opened.
All Fan cowl and Reverser latches opened.

sourceFile: 9. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B2 (50 Questions) R01.pdf
question: The warning message “Eng 1(2) Fan Cowl Not CLSD” come into view when: (CAT A, B1, B2)

options.text: Engine Fan cowl door latch No.1 opened.
At least 02 Fan cowl door latches opened.
All Fan cowl door latches opened.

sourceFile: 9. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B2 (50 Questions) R01.pdf
question: For CFM – LEAP 1A Engine, the Engine fan cowl door latches opening sequence is: (CAT A, B1, B2)

sourceFile: 9. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B2 (50 Questions) R01.pdf
question: How many holds open rod of Eng Leap 1-A nacelle: (CAT A, B1, B2)

options.text: 1 rod for Fan Cowl and 1 rod for Reverser
2 rod for Fan Cowl and 1 rod for Reverser
2 rod for Fan Cowl and 2 rod for Reverser

sourceFile: 9. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B2 (50 Questions) R01.pdf
question: How many strake installed on Engine Leap 1-A Fan Cowl door: (CAT A, B1, B2)

options.text: 1 for each Engine, on the right Fan cowl door
1 for each Engine, on the left Fan cowl door
2 for each Engine, 1 on each Fan cowl door

sourceFile: 9. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B2 (50 Questions) R01.pdf
question: What should you check before open the Engine Leap 1-A Fan cowl door: (CAT A, B1, B2)

options.text: Slat in fully retracted position (AMM 71-13-00-010-803)
Flap in fully retracted position
Parking brake applied

question: How many Fan Cowl Door latches are there on CFM-LEAP 1A? (CAT A, B1, B2)

sourceFile: 9. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B2 (50 Questions) R01.pdf
question: Bearing functions of LEAP-1A engine: (CAT B2)

options.text: Decrease the rolling friction, control the forces of gravity weight and the gyroscopic loads, aerodynamic loads of pumping and turbine driving
Decrease the rolling friction, control the forces of gravity weight and the linear loads, aerodynamic loads of pumping and turbine driving
Decrease the rolling friction, support the rotors axially, control the forces of gravity weight and the linear loads, aerodynamic loads of pumping and turbine driving

sourceFile: 9. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B2 (50 Questions) R01.pdf
question: Visual Inspection of the Fan Rotor Blades after Engine Operation in Severe Icing Condition: (CAT B2)

options.text: The leading edge of fan Inlet Guide Vanes (IGVs) and Outlet Guide Vanes (OGVs), the front acoustic panel, the flow splitter and the abradable flowpath
The leading edge of fan Inlet Guide Vanes (IGVs) and Outlet Guide Vanes (OGVs), the rear acoustic panel, the abradable flowpath, the flow splitter and flowpath panel
The leading edge of fan Outlet Guide Vanes (OGVs) and Inlet Guide Vanes (IGVs), the front acoustic panel, the flow splitter and flowpath panel

sourceFile: 9. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B2 (50 Questions) R01.pdf
question: The IGVs at the inlet to the HPC are: (CAT B2)

options.text: Used to convert air pressure increase into velocity rise and direct air from the booster to the stage 1 high pressure compressor stator blades
Used to convert air pressure increase into velocity rise and direct air from the booster to the stage 1 high pressure compressor rotor blades
C. Used to convert air velocity increase into pressure rise and direct air from the booster to the stage 1 high pressure compressor rotor blades

sourceFile: 9. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B2 (50 Questions) R01.pdf
question: Fault of the Bowed Rotor Overspeed Start Abort on Engine 1(2) on Channel A or Channel B:(CAT B2)

options.text: No troubleshooting is necessary
Do a borescope inspection of HPC stage 5 and stage 9, Check for concurrent fault in the PFR,EEC LAST LEG REPORT, SMR Report, Class 3 report related to Variable Stator Vane (VSV)
Do a borescope inspection of HPC stage 4 and stage 9, Check for concurrent fault in the PFR, EEC LAST LEG REPORT, the engine automatic start

sourceFile: 9. LEAP-1A Engine  CAT B2 (50 Questions) R01.pdf
question: Inspection after Engine Windmilling with the operational limits have not been exceeded: (CAT B2)

options.text: Inspect the Oil Filter, the Oil Debris Monitoring System Sensor and the AGB and TGB sumps scavenge screen plugs
Inspect the Oil Filter, the A, B and C sumps, and the LPT and TGB sumps scavenge screen plugs
Inspect the Oil Filter, the Oil Debris Monitoring System Sensor, the A, B and C sumps, and the AGB and TGB sumps scavenge screen plugs

No renewal is allowed.

Dry the remaining fuel that can collect in the combustion chamber.

installed at 6:30 o'clock on the accessory gearbox (AGB) rear face.

fitted on the fan hub with two bolts located on the n8 strut.
installed in the fan frame strut No.6 at the 5:00 o'clock position.

to the junction box by a flexible harness.
